title: Infering DSP with signed input and output
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using Yosys tag `yosys-0.9` I can't infer DSP tiles in this case:

```verilog
// yosys -p 'synth_ice40 -dsp' multiplier.v

module multiplier #(
	parameter BITSIZE = 16,
)(	
    input wire lrclk,
    input wire signed [BITSIZE-1:0] in1,
    input wire signed [BITSIZE-1:0] in2,
    output reg signed [BITSIZE-1:0] out,
);

always @(posedge lrclk) begin
    out <= in1 * in2;
end

endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

I have seen that that `SB_MAC16` is able to multiply two signed  16 bits integer in this document: [Lattice DSP Guide](http://www.latticesemi.com/-/media/LatticeSemi/Documents/UserManuals/1D/DSPGuideforFPGAs.ashx?document_id=20342)
I would like to obtain the same synthesis result as if I remove the input/output `signed`:

```
   Number of wires:                  9
   Number of wire bits:             69
   Number of public wires:           5
   Number of public wire bits:      50
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                  1
     SB_MAC16                        1
```


## Actual behavior

It does not:

```
   Number of wires:                311
   Number of wire bits:            386
   Number of public wires:           5
   Number of public wire bits:      50
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                344
     SB_CARRY                        8
     SB_DFF                         16
     SB_LUT4                       320
```

Thanks!
